text: ‘Do not let anybody have a claim on you’, etc., this is usually explained as meaning that we are to be diligent in paying off our debts, except that debt of mutual love, which can never be paid off, but always urges. In view of what immediately follows, it seems likely St Paul also means, that we shall seldom have scruples of conscience over the obligations of human justice, if we live by the law of divine love.
The connexion of this passage with what goes before is only general; it is more closely related to what follows, viz. the anxiety of St Paul over the want of unity among Christians at Rome.
